blocks.content: The gathered guards felt the world sway with each step Kuan took.
His steps were neither fast nor slow, almost pitiful in a way.
It's tilting.
It felt like being on a ship in a storm, hard to keep their balance.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: Hmm.
Natasha touched her lips with an intrigued expression, while the Wind Guards looked displeased.
Leader Yula spoke.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: Block his approach.
Among all the murderous intent directed at them, the strongest was aimed at the Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guards
blocks.content: Yes.
Just as 99 swordsmen were about to scatter like the wind, Kido stepped forward.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Stop.
Kuan halted.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: You're a swordsman from Tormia? This is a restricted area. You can't enter without a permit. Go back.
Tormia couldn't be hated.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I have business at the main building.
Kido glanced at Delta's building and then back at Kuan, shaking his head.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Then I can't let you through. Once the Holy War begins, no one can enter or leave.
Rai interjected.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: He can enter. He's part of Tormia's special forces and has a pass.
Kido bit his lip.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He has killing intent but no hostility. If that changes, it would be terrifying…
If he had a pass, it might be fine to let him through.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: He must be killed.
Yula was resolute.
The Wind Guards, though not as close as Kido, was also in a position to gauge Uorin's intentions.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guards
blocks.content: That man is a threat to the Empress. This might be a good chance. Killing him here won't affect the Holy War.
Kuan had crossed the line.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: I don't know why you're late, but this is a restricted area. You can't go through here.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Then where should I go?

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: That's up to you. But the only way to Delta's main building is through here.
They intended to trap him.
Knowing Yula's nature, Kido pleaded with Kuan.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Go back, please.
Kuan calmly met his gaze and murmured.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: You're lucky.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Huh?
The ground suddenly tilted sharply towards Kuan, creating a slope over 70 degrees.
Simultaneously, the guards expelled external force.

blocks.speaker: Guards
blocks.content: Catch him!
Natasha fell naturally, Kido stayed grounded, and the Wind Guards flew like the wind, drawing their sword.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guards
blocks.content: Can't let him escape.
Kuan seemed to fall off a cliff.
Losing sight of him for even a moment would be the end.
Then the 70-degree tilt flipped to the left.

blocks.speaker: Guards
blocks.content: Damn it!
North and south reversed again, now spinning like a sphere.
Only Kido, grounded like a spirit, could track Kuan's movements.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: Phew! This is really strange.
Meanwhile, Natasha enjoyed the spinning world like a ride.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: It's not easy.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Using the initial tilt on a single target made it easy to escape their sight.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But it's hard to shake them all off.
Hundreds of swordsmen, all top-tier in the kingdom, were present.
A member of the Wind Guards reported to Yula.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Member
blocks.content: Captain, he might have a plan. Moving as a group could create gaps in our defense…
The fact that everyone was being led by a single swordsman was humiliating enough.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: But he's that kind of man.
She remembered young Kuan, crawling up a mountain path with a torn Achilles tendon.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: You've come this far.
Suppressing the urge to applaud him as a swordsman,
she gave her order.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: End it in one go. Form the death formation.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guards
blocks.content: Yes.
The 100 members of the Wind Guards crossed like a net and began rotating around Kuan.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: If caught, not even bones will remain.
It wasn't an exaggeration.
But Kuan, facing the best swordsmen of Kashan, looked colder than ever.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I'm just doing what I must.
He refused the position of guard captain because protecting someone didn't suit him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I am a clown.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: A pierrot who could do any foolish thing to make her laugh.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: What was I expecting?
Still human, did he fantasize about being with her?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Heh.
In the dark curtain of the Wind Guards tightening net,
he thought of Siina's face.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Smile.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Because it's fun.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guards
blocks.content: Strike!
A hundred blades created a storm, devastating the spot where Kuan stood.
The landscape returned to normal, and the Wind Guards members reformed their formation.
Kido looked dazed.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He's gone.
He didn't see it.
In an instant, the scene distorted, and Kuan's body vanished.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Move.
Rai rushed over.
At the center of the swirling grass, red drops of blood fell.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Did we cut him?
Yula didn't lie.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: No. None of us cut him. He disappeared just before our swords reached him.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Then this blood?

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: Probably…
Yula pointed to her head with her finger.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: His ears.
By rupturing his own eardrums, his hearing was now half-gone.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Mr. Kuan.
Facing the situation where he single-handedly broke through the guards of 12 countries.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Are you truly becoming a ghost?
Rai had a glimpse of what one must sacrifice to become the strongest swordsman.
The Pope delivered a speech.

blocks.speaker: Pope
blocks.content: Lamiism is a global religion. However, not everyone in the world believes in Lamiism.
The atmosphere was solemn.

blocks.speaker: Pope
blocks.content: Faith always comes with a price. It is an act of burying the soul and knowing that there is a higher transcendence beyond any reason.
The Pope's gaze turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Pope
blocks.content: What can be gained from that price is strength. The peace that comes from believing that I am not my own. Isn't that what this world needs?
His gaze shifted away.

blocks.speaker: Pope
blocks.content: The value of Lamiism lies in the courage, sacrifice, and hope that those who cannot fight can lean on.
Gis murmured.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Oh, God.

blocks.speaker: Pope
blocks.content: No one is a god. As the Pope of Lamiism, I earnestly pray to the God of Cria that a Leadership Council will emerge to fight for all humanity.
Applause filled the conference hall.
For the Kingdom of Tormia, which relied on the power of Yahweh, it was an uncomfortable situation.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: It can't be helped.
Lupist leaned his temple on his fist.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: As the official religion of the Holy War Leadership Council, it has a strong influence. Above all, it has a justification that the kingdom lacks.
Plu agreed.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: It's not blatant, but they're subtly using wording that denies Yahweh. It's intentional.
Everyone's inner thoughts might differ, but officially, the atmosphere was one of admiration.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Next, we will begin the proposal from the International Tribunal.
Chief Socrates, a large, obese man, climbed onto the podium, wiping his sweat.

blocks.speaker: Socrates
blocks.content: Ugh. Phew, it's hot. Or is it just me? Wearing this fat suit.
No one responded, so he burst into a near-shout of laughter.

blocks.speaker: Socrates
blocks.content: Just kidding! Hahaha!
The atmosphere was the worst.

blocks.speaker: Socrates
blocks.content: Ahem. Then I will propose. I, Chief of the International Tribunal, Bebeto Socrates…
He glared and spat out.

blocks.speaker: Socrates
blocks.content: Resign as of today.
While the officials from various countries had no reaction, those working in the Holy War were in an uproar.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Mr. Socrates, what do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Socrates
blocks.content: Exactly what I said. Resign. I'd rather eat another loaf of bread. Eating is what remains. I recommend Mito Shirano as my successor. That's all.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Shirano?
Socrates stepped down, and a thin, middle-aged woman climbed onto the podium.
Nicknamed the Swamp Witch, she was once the head of the Magic Association in the Kingdom of Stang.

blocks.speaker: Shirano
blocks.content: Thank you, Mr. Socrates, for passing on this crappy position to me.
True to her character, her words were harsh.

blocks.speaker: Shirano
blocks.content: Then I'll propose. I resign as of this moment.

blocks.speaker: Officials
blocks.content: What is this! Is the Holy War a joke!
Finally, some officials protested, but Shirano was unfazed.

blocks.speaker: Shirano
blocks.content: Shut up. Don't look down on mages, and don't drag us into this childish political game. Everyone knows, right?
In the ensuing silence, the chairman spoke.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: But… if you resign, there will be no one to make proposals. Isn't it courteous to present an agenda before resigning?

blocks.speaker: Shirano
blocks.content: Proposal.
Shirano spat out.

blocks.speaker: Shirano
blocks.content: The strong eat the weak. That's all.
As she stepped down, the politicians were stunned, but some smiled.
They were mages.

blocks.speaker: Smiling Politicians
blocks.content: They have their own stubbornness. They never swallow what they find repulsive.
King Munryong of the Kingdom of Mun stood up.

blocks.speaker: King Munryong
blocks.content: This is outrageous! To raise the banner of survival of the fittest at a gathering for world peace!
The chairman tried to calm him.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Please calm down. It's just the words of a single chief. Besides, she's no longer a chief…

blocks.speaker: King Munryong
blocks.content: Do you think that will work? The International Tribunal oversees all the rules and laws of the Holy War!
The intensity of his words was strong.

blocks.speaker: King Munryong
blocks.content: First, they allow the villain Havitz to attend the Holy War, and now they say the strong eat the weak. Does the Holy War want to smell blood? If taken literally, it means they won't intervene even if a king is beheaded!

blocks.speaker: Observers
blocks.content: That's too much!
Mentioning the assassination of a king made even the observers react sensitively.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: This is a gathering for peace. Isn't King Mun, who harbors such vile thoughts, the one with ulterior motives?

blocks.speaker: King Munryong
blocks.content: What did you say? How dare you…!
The topic was so sensitive that everyone stood up, and the atmosphere of the Holy War turned combative.

blocks.speaker: Observer/Politician
blocks.content: Assassination attempts will happen anyway. The side that loses here will be the first to get hit.
Amidst the boasting, bluffing, and threats, the door to the conference hall opened.
The mere fact that the door opened surprised everyone, and silence fell instantly.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: What, what is this?
A one-armed swordsman bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Sorry if I interrupted.
As he limped down the stairs, the onlookers were even more bewildered.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Mr. Kuan.
Siena watched as the chairman stopped Kuan.
Her emotions were mixed.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Wait. Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I am Parka Kuan of the Tormia Kingdom's special forces. I was late due to circumstances.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Are you serious…
The chairman was more curious than scolding.
He stopped and asked again.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: How did you get in here?
Once the conference begins, the guards of each country guard the only exit.
Kuan looked back at the door and answered.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Walked in…

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: That's not what I mean! How did you get in? All the doors of the main building are closed, and you can't enter without passing the guards…
At that moment, the chairman realized.

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Could it be?

blocks.speaker: Chairman
blocks.content: Did he break through the guards of 12 countries?
At that moment, blood gushed from one of Kuan's ears and fell to the ground.
Siena's eyes widened, and everyone who knew him looked shocked.

blocks.speaker: Observer/Siena
blocks.content: Pressure. He sacrificed one ear.
The realization made the audience fall silent as Kuan walked again.
He stopped at Tormia's council.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Excuse me.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: Oh, yes.
Quick-witted Thad moved aside, and Kuan slowly sat in his chair.
Next to Siena.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Why…?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: This is my seat.
Albino and Uorin knew what Kuan intended.

blocks.speaker: Albino and Uorin
blocks.content: The country that touches Siena… will be the first to die.
The unspoken threat weighed heavily on the room.
Albino nodded.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: If he can't protect her, he'll cut them all down. If he's truly given up everything…
Kuan was now a sword demon.
